title: How Super El Niño Hits Power Markets: Analyst Reaction (Podcast)
summary: El Niño has officially arrived, and there are concerns this time that the weather phenomenon might be more intense than most years, a so-called “super” event. With Pacific Ocean temperatures now exceeding the threshold for one of the strongest forms of the weather pattern, the risk of prolonged heat, drought, low wind and other disruptive weather events is rising across multiple regions at once. For commodity and power markets, that can mean greater volatility, from wind droughts in Europe and h

title: UK in Effort to Improve Construction Workers’ Mental Health Amid Out-of-Work Crisis
summary: The UK’s business department is spearheading a new effort to improve mental health in the construction industry, as the government tries to tackle a labor market crisis that’s been worsened by Britain’s high numbers of people out of work due to mental illness.

title: Waste Management of Canada Eyes Selling C$750 Million of Bonds
summary: Waste Management of Canada is looking to sell as much as C$750 million ($536 million) of notes as soon as this week, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: S&P 500 Set for Best Day Since April as Oil Sinks: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks climbed around the world, joining gains in bonds as oil slumped after the US and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, fueling hopes for an end to the conflict that has roiled markets.

title: SpaceX Jumps 11% in Second Day of Trading After Record IPO
summary: SpaceX shares jumped in their second day of trading, adding to gains following a blockbuster debut that instantly vaulted it into the ranks of the world’s most valuable public companies.

title: US Stocks Rally on Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz; SpaceX Gains
summary: US stocks rose sharply after the US and Iran reached an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, sending oil prices lower and easing concern about inflation ahead of Kevin Warsh’s first meeting as chairman of the Federal Reserve this week. SpaceX shares advanced, helping boost risk-on sentiment.

title: Banks Vie to Underwrite £5 Billion Intertek Buyout Debt
summary: Bankers are lining up debt packages of about £5 billion ($6.7 billion) to back a potential takeover of British product-testing firm Intertek Group Plc by private equity firm EQT AB.

title: Montreal Transit System REM to Sell Around C$2 Billion Bonds
summary: Réseau express métropolitain (REM), an automated light metro system in Greater Montreal, is looking to raise around C$2 billion ($1.43 million) in a bond sale as soon as this week, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Aluminum Slumps as Iran Peace Deal Softens Blow of Supply Crisis
summary: Aluminum slumped to the lowest in more than two months as an interim agreement between the US and Iran laid the groundwork for the resumption of metal shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US Manufacturing Output Stalls for the First Time This Year
summary: US manufacturing production stalled in May after four months of gains as a drop in chemical and petroleum output masked ongoing strength in categories tied to the data center boom.

title: Nuveen Private Bond Chief Scoops Up AI’s ‘Picks and Shovels’
summary: Data centers and computing chips are all the rage in global credit markets right now, but Nuveen LLC’s Laura Parrott is more excited about the “boring, plain vanilla” infrastructure on the outskirts of AI that will benefit regardless of how the technology pans out.

title: Knicks Win, Giving Tortured Fans First NBA Title in 53 Years
summary:
The New York Knicks ended one of the longest championship droughts in the NBA, bringing relief to their long-suffering fans.

On Saturday night, the franchise won its first title since 1973, and third all-time, with a 94-90 win at the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5. 

Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern was at the game and talks about the experience. 

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: IMF Says Still on ‘High Alert’ for War Impact After Iran-US Deal
summary: The International Monetary Fund said it remains on “high alert” over fallout from the Middle East war on the global economy, warning that energy supplies will take time to recover even as the US and Iran announced an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gold Jumps After US and Iran Reach Deal to Reopen Hormuz Strait
summary: Gold and silver rallied after the US and Iran reached an interim deal to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing global inflation fears and potentially tempering expectations for interest-rate hikes.

title: Aerospace Supplier Doncasters Seeks $746.7 Million in US IPO
summary: Doncasters Group, a manufacturer of products for aerospace engines and industrial gas turbines, is seeking to raise as much as $746.7 million in an initial public offering, joining a wave of aerospace and defense companies tapping public markets amid rising demand for aircraft and military equipment.

title: Nvidia Joins AI Debt Boom With Bond Sale Targeting $20 Billion
summary: Chipmaking giant Nvidia Corp. is looking to sell at least $20 billion of bonds on Monday, joining a wave of companies that are borrowing as they pay for investments in the artificial intelligence boom.

title: Warsh Caught Between Trump, Bond Market Bet on Rate Hikes
summary:
Just three weeks into the job, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is already facing an unusually high-stakes test.

Inflation is roaring back at the fastest pace in three years. There’s growing dissent among the central bank’s policymakers. And investors have been dumping US Treasury bonds and piling into bets the Fed will need to start raising rates by December, defying President Trump’s call to lower them instead. 

We look ahead to this week's FOMC decision with Rebecca Patterson, Senior Fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations & former Chief Investment Strategist at Bridgewater Associates. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: What the US-Iran Deal Means for the Strait of Hormuz
summary:
Oil prices tumbled after the US and Iran agreed an interim peace deal — but key questions about the Strait of Hormuz remain.

Alex Longley explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Sinks as US-Iran Deal Boosts Outlook for Reopening of Hormuz
summary: Oil sank after the US and Iran agreed to an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, though shipowners were still hunting for more details before committing to resuming transits.

title: Fertilizer Ships Face Long Backlog Even If Hormuz Reopens
summary: An interim US-Iran deal to end their months-long war and potentially fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz is unlikely to immediately ease fertilizer flows as shipowners wait for more details to assess the safety of transits.

title: Europe’s Alternative to Patriot Missile Is Finding More Buyers
summary: More nations are evaluating the European-made SAMP/T NG missile defense system, demonstrating the Franco-Italian interceptor’s emergence as an alternative to hard-to-get Patriot batteries from the US.

title: Said’s Al Shams Accuses Braemar Hotels of ‘Self-Dealing’
summary: Businessman Wafic Rida Said has criticized Braemar Hotels & Resorts Inc. over a series of recent transactions that amount to “a brazen act of self-dealing,” according to a letter sent to the real estate investment trust.

title: US Bonds Rally as Traders Trim Fed Rate-Hike Bets on Iran Deal
summary: Treasuries advanced as investors dialed back expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes following news of a deal to halt the Iran war.

title: Thousands of Public-School Staff Across US Face Layoff Warnings
summary: Thousands of US public-school employees have received warnings saying they could lose their jobs as the nation’s education system grapples with mounting financial pressures.

title: Airport Handler Menzies Sees High Jet Fuel Prices Through Summer
summary: Airport ground-services provider Menzies Aviation Ltd. expects jet fuel costs to stay high for several more months because of fallout from the Iran war.

title: Euro Area Is Seeing Ratings Increasingly Converge, Scope Says
summary: The euro area is experiencing a phase of convergence across the spectrum of national credit scores despite wildly varying underlying dynamics, Scope Ratings said.

title: World Bank Makes Second Foray Into Emerging-Market CLO Funding
summary: The World Bank’s loan-backed bond program has surpassed the $1 billion mark after the lender raised another $509 million for a program aimed at supporting job creation in developing nations.

title: Nuvei Agrees to Buy Payoneer in $2.75 Billion Payments Deal
summary: Nuvei Corp. has agreed to buy Payoneer Global Inc. for roughly $2.75 billion, forging a deal that would combine two North American payments companies that process more than half a trillion dollars in annual volume.

title: Why Solar Power Belongs on Earth
summary: Orbiting solar farms may sound like the future of clean energy. Bloomberg Opinion’s Mark Gongloff explains why keeping renewables on Earth is the simpler and smarter option. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EE.UU. e Irán acuerdan detener guerra y reanudar envíos de petróleo en Medio Oriente
summary: E.UU. e Irán sellan un acuerdo provisional para reabrir el estrecho de Ormuz, poniendo fin a una guerra que dejó miles de muertos y abriendo 60 días de negociaciones nucleares.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Activist Investor Ananym Urges Bio-Techne to Consider Selling
summary: Activist investor Ananym Capital Management has built a stake in Bio-Techne Corp. and is pushing the board to conduct a strategic review that would include a potential sale.

title: AMD Deal Helps Austrian Chip-Component Maker Extend 500% Rally
summary: Shares in chip-part maker Austria Technologie & Systemtechnik AG rallied the most in six years after the company raised its guidance and pledged increased output and investment as part of a deal to supply Advanced Micro Devices Inc.
